summary: Six distinct criteria—color changes, transparency issues, chalkiness or banding marks, stain presence, pit formations, and texture irregularities—are individually rated using values between 0-5 under Modified Dean's Fluorosis Index, leading to an aggregate evaluation reflecting various aspects of dental health affected by excess fluoride intake. This multi-faceted approach ensures thorough documentation of both aesthetic impacts and potential functional impairments associated with fluorotic conditions across all examined teeth surfaces during examination procedures conducted systematically among patients suspected at risk based upon historical records environmental exposures current oral hygiene regimens diet composition medical history
